replication_study.hypothesis: In PISA 2012 data, the interaction between students’ use of memorization (standardized) and school-average mathematics ability (standardized school-level mean of plausible values) is negative in its association with standardized mathematics self-concept.

data_plan.wave_or_subset: 2012 wave; multi-country sample (countries identified by CNT)
data_plan.sample_size: Max N = 480,174 rows loaded; non-missing MEMOR = 309,584. Final sample size will be determined after exclusions for missing codes, nonresponse, and school size > 10.
data_plan.unit_of_analysis: Individual student (students nested in schools nested in countries)
data_plan.access_details: Provided locally as data/original/2/input/replication_data/PISA2012.replication.RDS; will be mounted to /app/data for analysis.

qualification.explanation: The replication uses a different PISA wave (2012) than the original (2003), preserving measures, structure, and operationalization while providing an independent sample to test robustness.
qualification.similarity_to_original: Both use PISA data with: math self-concept (SCMAT), five plausible values for math ability (PV1MATH–PV5MATH), school identifiers (SCHOOLID), country identifiers (CNT), and student weights (W_FSTUWT). The replication code follows the same multilevel structure and PISA pooling procedures (see !!CORRECTION!!_Seaton_AmEduResJourn_2010_Blxd_final.R). Original details confirm BFLPE modeling with plausible values, standardized variables, and weights (post_registration.json, method section). The focal moderator is memorization (MEMOR), present in the replication dataset (columns list).
qualification.deviation_from_original: Original used PISA 2003 across 41 countries (post_registration.json: data.source and wave_or_subset); replication uses PISA 2012 (file name and columns in PISA2012.replication.RDS). The replication dataset’s MEMOR variable is observed for 309,584 students out of 480,174 total rows (dataset info). Any minor instrument revisions between 2003 and 2012 may affect scale properties. Country composition and sample sizes likely differ across waves.

data_plan.notes: PISA uses complex survey design; the provided code applies student weights (W_FSTUWT) but does not incorporate replicate weights/jackknife, which may affect SEs. Missing data are handled using PISA missing codes (<= 997) and listwise deletion. The script drops schools with n <= 10. Variables are standardized within the pooled dataset. Outputs are saved to /app/data.

steps: Load PISA2012.replication.RDS from /app/data and create unique school and student IDs.
Filter out missing/nonresponse using PISA codes (<= 997) for SCMAT, PV1-5MATH, MEMOR; enforce non-NA.
Standardize SCMAT (outcome), PV1-5MATH (student ability predictors), MEMOR (moderator).
Compute school-average ability (mean of PVxMATH_z by school) for each plausible value.
Create interaction terms: MEMOR_z × school_PVxMATH_z for each plausible value (CROSS1–CROSS5).
Fit five multilevel models (lmer) for each plausible value with random intercepts for school and country, random slopes as specified, and student weights.
Extract the CROSS coefficient from each model, pool the estimate as the mean across plausible values; compute pooled SE using PISA manual formulas; combine p-values via Fisher’s method.
Save model summaries and pooled outputs to /app/data.
planned_method.models: For each plausible value x in {1..5}: lmer(SCMAT_z ~ PVxMATH_z + PVxMATH_z_sq + school_PVxMATH_z + MEMOR_z + CROSSx + (PVxMATH_z + PVxMATH_z_sq + MEMOR_z | uniqueSchoolID) + (PVxMATH_z + PVxMATH_z_sq + school_PVxMATH_z + MEMOR_z | CNT), weights = W_FSTUWT, REML = TRUE). Pool estimates across plausible values per PISA guidelines.
planned_method.independent_variables: PVxMATH_z (student math ability) and PVxMATH_z_sq (quadratic term), school_PVxMATH_z (school-average ability), MEMOR_z (memorization), and the focal moderator term CROSSx = MEMOR_z × school_PVxMATH_z.
planned_method.control_variables: Quadratic term of individual math ability; random intercepts for school and country; random slopes for specified predictors; student sampling weights (W_FSTUWT).
planned_method.tools_software: R (lme4, lmerTest, afex, dplyr, plyr, car, metaRNASeq, multtest via BiocManager, metap).

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: Pooled standardized interaction coefficient for MEMOR_z × school_PVxMATH_z across five plausible values and its pooled standard error.
planned_estimation_and_test.test: Two-sided significance via Fisher’s combined probability test over five models; additional t-based inference per lmer outputs.

planned_method.missing_data_handling: Listwise deletion after filtering PISA missing codes (<= 997) for SCMAT, PV1-5MATH, MEMOR; remove schools with n <= 10.
planned_method.multiple_testing_policy: Not applicable for a single focal moderator; p-values are combined across plausible values (not multiple outcomes).
planned_method.inference_criteria: Primary: interaction coefficient is negative and statistically significant (e.g., p < 0.001 as emphasized in the original due to large N). Secondary: magnitude comparable to original effect size direction (negative).

files.!!CORRECTION!!_Seaton_AmEduResJourn_2010: End-to-end replication script: loads PISA2012.replication.RDS from /app/data; constructs standardized variables; computes school-average ability by school; forms interaction terms; fits five multilevel lmer models (one per plausible value) with weights and specified random effects; pools coefficients and computes pooled SE per PISA manual; combines p-values via Fisher’s method; writes model summaries and pooled results to /app/data.
files.PISA2012.replication.RDS: Replication dataset with key columns: CNT, SCHOOLID, STIDSTD, W_FSTUWT, SCMAT, PV1MATH–PV5MATH, MEMOR (plus SES and other constructs). Serves as the analysis dataset.

codebase.notes: Edits made to the R script to comply with required I/O: replaced remote URL loading with local read from /app/data/PISA2012.replication.RDS; redirected outputs (model summaries and combined p-values CSV) to /app/data. Dependencies include lme4, lmerTest, afex, car, dplyr, plyr, metaRNASeq, multtest (Bioc), and metap.

instructions: Place PISA2012.replication.RDS in ./data (host), mounted to /app/data in the container.
Run: Rscript /app/data/original/2/input/replication_data/!!CORRECTION!!_Seaton_AmEduResJourn_2010_Blxd_final.R (or adjust path as appropriate in the container).
Outputs generated in /app/data: model1Output.txt ... model5Output.txt, cross_P_results.csv, plus printed pooled estimate, SE, and p-value.
Confirm that the pooled interaction (final_crossB) is negative and significant, and record its magnitude and SE.
comparison_metrics: Direction consistency: interaction (MEMOR × school-avg ability) should be negative (initial_details.txt; post_registration.json numerical_results).
Statistical significance: target p < 0.001 as in original due to large N (post_registration.json).

Magnitude comparison: compare pooled coefficient and derived effect size against original reported values (-0.089 coefficient and effect size ≈ -0.157; initial_details.txt; original_paper.pdf summary).
Robustness across plausible values: check sign consistency of CROSS1–CROSS5 estimates.
